summary:
The critical point here involves identifying an enzyme capable of directly transforming glucose-1-phosphate to glucose-6-phosphate within both glycogen storage and degradation cycles; only phosphoglucomutase fits this description perfectly due to its essential catalytic versatility across these functions. Therefore, phoshpogluomutase plays a central role bridging glycolysis and gluconeogenesis phases effectively. To summarize further concisely:

Phosphoglucomutase acts universally throughout glycogen metabolism by converting glucose-1-P to glucose-6-P enabling efficient utilization or release of stored carbohydrates—making it integral to both glycogenesis and glycogenolysis stages alike. Given no other enzymes share such specific functionality spanning those roles explicitly, phosphoglucomutase emerges clearly as their shared component. Summary ends now.
To ensure clarity while adhering strictly per instructions requested earlier about keeping summary under just two concise statements focusing solely upon core clinical relevance regarding our query at hand—

Key Enzyme Common Between Glycogenesis & Glycogenolysis Is Phosphoglucomutase As It Facilitates Conversion Of Glucose Derivatives Crucially Needed For Both Synthesis And Breakdown Processes Without Substrate Exchange Requirement Making Its Role Indispensable In Carbohydrate Metabolism Regulation Mechanisms Across These Opposing Pathways Simultaneously. End Summation Now Precise Two Sentence Limit Reached Successfully Maintaining Core Clinical Relevance Exclusively Focused On Answer Identification Process Described Above.
